mechanism_narrative: Month-on-month growth in retail and food services sales (RSAFS) above its 12-month moving average signals strengthening consumer demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as expected corporate revenues rise.
failure_condition: Rolling 20-day forward return correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth is non-positive over a 2-year window, or the signal fails to produce an information coefficient above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of at least 0.2 percentage points indicates labor market strengthening, which boosts consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE decreases by >= 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: If after a UNRATE decline >=0.2pp, the forward 5-day return is negative more than 50% of the time over any 3-year period, or the average return is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.25% (near zero) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) increase by more than 150k in a month, the combined signal of accommodative monetary policy and robust job growth strongly predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days, as the economy gains momentum without tightening risks.
failure_condition: If the joint condition produces negative or near-zero average forward 20-day returns over any 2-year rolling window, or if the interaction term in a regression is not statistically significant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) above 2% year-over-year signals a robust manufacturing sector, which historically boosts equity returns. However, during late-cycle expansions when capacity constraints emerge, further INDPRO growth may precede a downturn. In the current early-recovery regime (2012), positive INDPRO growth is bullish for S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If INDPRO YoY > 2% does not lead to positive average 60-day forward returns in the early-cycle regime (as identified by NBER recession end dates), or if the signal produces negative returns in >40% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The quarterly growth rate of real GDP (GDPC1) serves as a fundamental driver of equity valuations. A positive quarter-over-quarter growth rate signals economic expansion and supports S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, as corporate earnings rise.
failure_condition: If the correlation between quarterly GDP growth and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is non-positive over any 10-year period, or if the signal's information coefficient is below 0.02 over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1% (accommodative), it signals that monetary policy is supportive of asset prices. However, if the rate has remained unchanged for 3 months, the market may begin to anticipate tightening, creating a negative signal for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 1% and unchanged for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If the condition does not produce negative average 20-day returns over any 3-year period, or if the rate of false positives (negative signal but positive return) exceeds 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts (HOUST) reflect residential investment and consumer confidence. Sustained growth in HOUST (above its 6-month moving average) signals a healthy housing market, which supports consumer spending and equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the signal's correlation with forward 5-day returns is non-positive over a 5-year window, or if the average return difference between growth and decline periods is not significant at 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When both nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) increase by more than 200k and retail sales (RSAFS) grow month-over-month, it confirms broad-based economic momentum. This joint upward signal strongly predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. If only one component meets its threshold, the signal is weaker.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change > 200k AND RSAFS m/m growth > 0%
failure_condition: If the joint condition leads to negative average 20-day forward returns over any 2-year period, or if the interaction term in a regression does not improve predictive performance over individual signals (tested by out-of-sample R-squared).
